zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthull是Sir Richard De Harthill和Alice Astley的女兒，於1360年出生。她與Edmund Cokayne結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1417年之後在英國英格蘭德比夏爾阿希布恩，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthull是Sir Richard De Harthill和Alice Astley的女儿，于1360年出生。她与Edmund Cokayne结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1417年之后在英国英格兰德比夏爾阿希布恩，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harthull
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from any of three places called Harthill (Cheshire, Derbys, and WR Yorks).
2  Scottish: locative name from Harthill near Inverurie (Aberdeens) or, less likely, from the barony of Harthill (Lanarks).

variants.name: Hartell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Hartle  .
Americanized form of German Hartel or Härtel.

variants.name: Hartles
meanings: English: variant of Hartle ( see Harthill ) with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Hartill
meanings: English (Staffordshire) and Scottish: variant of   Hartle  .

variants.name: Hartle
meanings: German (also Härtle): from a pet form of the various ancient Germanic compound names formed with hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’ as the first element.

English (Worcestershire and Staffordshire): habitational name from any of the three places called Harthill (Cheshire, Derbyshire, and Yorkshire), named with Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + hyll ‘hill’.
Scottish: habitational name from Harthill near Inverurie (Aberdeenshire) or, less likely, from the barony of Harthill (Lanarkshire), probably from the same etymology as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthull was born in 1360 as the daughter of Sir Richard De Harthill and Alice Astley. She married Edmund Cokayne. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died after 1417, in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Polesworth, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthullは1360年にSir Richard De HarthillとAlice Astleyの娘として生まれた。Edmund Cokayneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1417年以後にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアアシュボウルンで死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアポレスウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.Ashborne, Derbyshire, Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 애스h보우른 위스 마플레턴
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthull(은)는 1360년 Sir Richard De Harthill(와)과 Alice Astley의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Edmund Cokayne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1417년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 애스h보우른에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 포레스워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthull nasceu em 1360 filha de Sir Richard De Harthill e Alice Astley. Ela casou-se com Edmund Cokayne. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1417, em Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Polesworth, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Ashborne, Derbyshire, Eng: ашбурн вит маплетон, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Harthull родилась в 1360 г. Её родителей звали Sir Richard De Harthill и Alice Astley. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Cokayne. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла после 1417 г. в местности ашбурн, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности полесворт, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
